characters.name: Abbie
characters.description: Not to be confused withAbbie Holiday.AbbieNameAbbieGenderFemaleHairBrownJobEmployee atKris' Kopy ShopDebut"The Accidental Terrorist"Voiced byAbby ElliottAbbieis an employee atKris' Kopy Shop. She only appears once in the Season 12 episode "The Accidental Terrorist". Abbie is a young Caucasian woman with a skinny build, pale skin, and long brown hair tied into a ponytail. She wears a yellow long-sleeved shirt, a blue work apron with an "Abbie" nametag, and blue jeans. AfterHank Hillwas accused of committing arson againstTom Hammond World of Cars, he went to Kris' Kopy Shop to findAlexsince Hank knew that he was the true culprit. Entering the shop, Hank asked the relaxed Abbie if she remembered a skinny man with a mustache beard and a mullet. Looking around, Abbie saw that there were many men fitting the description, so she asked Hank to be more specific with his description. She alleged that many of her customers had mustache beards due to the copy shop's proximity to a liberal arts college and a halfway house. Abbie remained silent as she watched OfficersBrownandFrankagain accuse Hank of leading the arson.

characters.name: Abbie Holiday
characters.description: Abbie HolidayNameAbbie HolidayGenderFemaleHairBlondeJobRealtor,Holiday and Sands RealtyDebut"The Redneck on Rainey Street"Voiced byPamela AdlonAbbie Holidayis the realtor forLuanne's old house (after she moved back in withHank). She only appears once in the Season 8 episode "The Redneck on Rainey Street". Abbie is a Caucasian woman with a skinny build, pale skin, and long blond hair. She wears a purple suit jacket and skirt, a lavender shirt, a brown purse, several articles of jewelry like gold bracelets and a necklace, and brown heels. Sometime afterLuannehad moved out of her old house adjacent to thealley, Abbie was assigned to sell the house. At the house, after she finished making a phone call, she walked up toHankand his friends and introduced herself with her business cards. She informed the men that their neighborhood was quite popular and complimented the curb appeal. Seeing Hank's house, Abbie praised the layout and advised him to give her a call if he ever thought about selling his home. In a short period of time,KahnandMinhlet their normally well-kept home fall into disrepair due to embracing the lifestyle of rednecks who lived elsewhere in the subdivision ofBelcher's Grove.

characters.name: Abraham Lincoln
characters.description: Abraham LincolnAnimatedRealNameAbraham LincolnGenderMaleHairBlackJobPresident of the United States of America(formerly, 1861-'65)Debut"The Man Who Shot Cane Skretteburg"Voiced byBreckin MeyerAbraham Lincoln(February 12, 1809 – April 15, 1865) was the 16th president of the United States, serving from 1861 until his assassination in 1865. He led the United States through the American Civil War, defeating the Confederate States of America and playing a major role in the abolition of slavery. Lincoln appeared in theSeason 12episode "Trans-Fascism" in which he was voiced byBreckin Meyer. In a flashback to how he andDidifirst met,Cottonexplains to another patient. He rhetorically asks if Lincoln asked for "girly gas" when he was assassinated. During a guilt induced dream,Hankfinds himself as a crew member on a Boeing B-17 bomber on a mission over Germany. When he reaches the cockpit, he discovers that the plane is being piloted byTom Landryand Abraham Lincoln, withGeorge Washingtonserving as the top turret Gunner. They express their concerns to Hank about his recent law breaking escapades requesting that he not do them. Hank retorts that the three of them broke the rules in some capacity, pointing out Lincoln's 1863 suspension of Habeas Corpus, to which he simply shrugs at. Suddenly, Lincoln spots an approaching BF 109, and swerves to avoid it, while Washington opens fire on it. Shortly after, Hank wakes up.

characters.name: Ace
characters.description: AceNameAceGenderMaleHairBlondJobExhibitionist baseball player for the Ace of Diamonds & His JewelsRelativesUnnamed wifeDebut"You Gotta Believe (In Moderation)"Voiced byJohn SchneiderTheAceis a baseball player who is the leader of an exhibitionist baseball team, the Ace of Diamonds and his Jewels. He only appears once in the Season 10 episode "You Gotta Believe (In Moderation)". Ace is a Caucasian man with a skinny and athletic build, tan skin, and short blond hair. He wears a red baseball cap with a three-dot dice symbol, a yellow baseball jersey with his name, white pants, and black baseball cleats. Ace plays other baseball teams for the purpose of entertainment rather than a serious game. His routine is to have the other team agree to lose to his team, and in return he donates the winning money to charity and various organizations. InArlen,Hank Hilland his team The Zephyrs call Ace's team and advertise the game. On game night, Ace is confused that Hank and his team are playing seriously rather than failing funnily, so Ace plays a serious game and easily beats their team. When the $9,300 check is presented to theTom Landry Middle Schoolbaseball team, Ace spitefully takes the check instead of giving it to the kids.

characters.name: Acne Pete
characters.description: Acne PeteNameAcne PeteGenderMaleHairOrangeJobStudent atTom Landry Middle SchoolDebut"The Boy Can't Help It"Voiced byUnknownAcne Peteis a student atTom Landry Middle School. He is perceived by other students to be a nerd. Pete is a young Caucasian boy with a skinny build, pale skin, short, upright, curly orange hair, buck teeth, and pimples across his face. He wears thick-framed glasses, an untucked, lime-green long-sleeved shirt, dark-tan pants, and black sneakers. In "I'm with Cupid", on the Valentine's Day flower sale at school, female students were encouraged to put flowers onto the male students whom they liked. WhenBobby Hillarrived at school, he noted that Acne Pete, who was perceived as a nerd by others, had a flower donned on him, indicating better luck for Bobby.

characters.name: Adrian Young
characters.description: Adrian YoungAnimatedRealNameAdrian Samuel YoungGenderMaleHairBlond (natural); Red (dyed)Age31(Season 5)[1]39(Season 13)[2]JobDrummer forNo DoubtDebut"Kidney Boy and Hamster Girl: A Love Story"Voiced byHimselfAdrian Young(born August 26, 1969) is an American musician, best known as the drummer of the rock bandsNo Doubtand Dreamcar. Young is a member of No Doubt with lead vocalsGwen Stefani, guitaristTom Dumont, and bassistTony Kanal. 39(Season 13)[2] Young guest starred and voiced himself in theSeason 5episode "Kidney Boy and Hamster Girl: A Love Story".

characters.name: Akihito
characters.description: AkihitoAnimatedRealNameTsugunomiya AkihitoGenderMaleHairDark grayAge68 (Season 6)JobEmperor of Japan (1989 - 2019)Emperor Emeritus of Japan (2019 - present)RelativesMichiko Shōda(wife)Naruhito(son)Debut"Returning Japanese II"Voiced byNoneAkihito(Japanese: 明仁) (born December 23, 1933) was the 125thEmperor of Japan, reigning from 1989 until his abdication in 2019. He currently carries the ceremonial title ofEmperor Emperitus. A fictional version of him appears once in a non-speaking role in theSeason 6episode "Returning Japanese II". Naruhito(son) Akihito is an adult Japanese man with a fit build, tan skin, and short dark gray hair. He wears a black suit, a white dress shirt, and a white necktie. He has on a gold and white sash work under his black suit jacket. He has on a red and gold medallion on a gold chain around his shoulders.

He has on a gold and white sash work under his black suit jacket. He has on a red and gold medallion on a gold chain around his shoulders.

characters.name: Alabaster Jones
characters.description: Alabaster JonesGenderMaleHairBrownJobPimpDebut"Ho Yeah!"Voiced bySnoop DoggAlabaster Jonesis a pimp who lives in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma. He was guest voiced by rapperSnoop Doggin his debut. Tammi Duvallhad worked under Alabaster in Oklahoma. When she moved in with the Hills in "Ho Yeah!", Alabaster believes thatHank Hillis a pimp who has stolen her, so he confronts him at hishouseand demands her return. Hank goes to the library to tell Tammi about Alabaster's presence, showingPeggythat she was a prostitute. As Hank drives Tammi back to his house, Alabaster rams into Hank's car, ensuing in a car chase. In the chase, Alabaster loses one of his expensive car's hubcaps. Soon, Hank stops the car but tells Tammi she will not go back and he will confront Alabaster himself. Hank puts on his cowboy hat and exits the car, giving Alabaster the money that Tammi earned from her recent side prostitution. Alabaster believes that Hank is shorting him on the amount of money Tammi earned, but Hank angrily says he is telling the truth. Alabaster is intimidated by Hank and decides to leave, but not before offering Hank to buy the "jasper brunette" woman in Hank's car, who is actually Peggy. Hank angrily states that Peggy is his wife, and Alabaster laughs since he believes a pimp's worst mistake is "marrying one of his hoes." He drives a highly ornamented Excalibur Series V and wears expensive and stylish clothing. Alabaster had a small cameo as a pimp being beaten by Virtual Hank of the video game in the episode "Grand Theft Arlen".

characters.name: Alex
characters.description: AlexNameAlexGenderMaleHairBlondDebut"The Accidental Terrorist"Voiced byDavid HermanAlexis a college student who wanted to helpHank HillprotestTom Hammond'scar business. He appears in the episode "The Accidental Terrorist". Alex is a young adult male with a skinny build, pale skin, shaggy blond hair, and a large goatee. WhenHankis atKris' Kopy Shopprinting out copies of a protest flyer, Alex sees the writing and agrees with Hank thatTom Hammondis one of many people ruining the world. Alex says he wants to help Hank, who tells him that he can meet him at Tom Hammond'sbusinessat 5 a.m., to which Alex hesitates but agrees.

She appears as an antagonist in the episode “The Powder Puff Boys.” Alexis is a woman in her 30s with long brown hair. She wears white-framed sunglasses, dress shirts and skirts.

characters.name: Alice
characters.description: AliceNameAliceGenderFemaleHairBrownDebut"The Substitute Spanish Prisoner"Voiced byBrittany MurphyAliceis a woman who was conned byDr. Robert Vayzosainto buying an illegitimate PhD. She only appears once in the Season 6 episode "The Substitute Spanish Prisoner". Alice is a young Caucasian woman with pale skin and long brown hair. She wears a green turtleneck sweater, a dark green dress, and brown shoes. When she posed as a betting shop cashier, she wore a red usher vest, a white dress shirt, and a black bowtie. On the internet, she took a phony PhD test on the website of the Texas Intelligence Institute, a fake educational institution created byDr. Robert Vayzosa. As part of Vayzosa's scam, he sent a letter in the mail to all people who took the test, stating that they were geniuses who were invited to a ceremony honoring the "150 smartest people in Texas". At theHotel Arlen, Alice received an "Alice" nametag and sat in the hotel's auditorium, sitting next toJimmy Wichard. She listened to Vayzosa's speech commending them for their supposed intelligence, and she was amazed at his proposal that they could buy a $1,000 book that would certify them to have a PhD. At first, Alice was disheartened that there was a catch but was relieved upon hearing that the "catch" was just being a genius. After socializing with other attendees, she bought a PhD book for herself, thinking that she would become a PhD holder once she finished the book and its lessons.

characters.description: Alla LubeckiNameAlla LubeckiGenderFemaleHairBrownRelativesNiefko Lubecki(husband)DebutThe Father, the Son, and J.C.Voiced byN/A Alla Lubeckiis a woman who fled with her husbandNiefkofrom an unspecified European communist country to the United States. She appears once in the episode "The Father, the Son, and J.C." Alla is an adult woman with brown hair tied into a ponytail in the back. She wears a dark purple and light purpled striped sweater with black jeans.

characters.name: Ally
characters.description: AllyNameAllyGenderFemaleHairBrownRelativesUnnamed sonDebut"Three Coaches and a Bobby"Voiced byJulie HagertyAllyis a soccer mom who frequently attended her son's soccer practices and matches. She only appears once in the Season 3 episode "Three Coaches and a Bobby". Ally is a Caucasian woman with a skinny build, pale skin, and short brown hair. She wears a cream-colored turtleneck sweater, light-cyan sweatpants, and blue shoes. WhenPeggysupportedBobby's decision to joinColeman Lucas's soccer team, Ally and the other soccer moms allowed her to sit with them in the bleachers during practice sessions. However, Ally quickly began to feel awkward with Peggy's demeanor and clothing. In one instance, she offered a latte to Peggy, who wore a plaid jacket instead of a stereotypical "soccer mom" sweater. Seeing Peggy with coffee on her upper lip, Ally politely signaled the appearance to her. Because Peggy noticed the moms' nonverbal signals that she should have worn a sweater, she eventually wore one to appease the women. Ally and the mothers saw Peggy come to them and do a Fat Albert impression, but they were unamused. The mothers commented that it was good Peggy wore what they deemed to be better clothing than her previous attire. Hearing Peggy explain that her husbandHankwas arriving with Bobby in their truck, Ally was disgusted that she drove a pickup truck instead of an SUV. Soon, as the boys' practice session continued, Ally spoke with another mother about how SUVs were far superior to pickup trucks, reasoning that being in an SUV would increase her chances of surviving a crash. No longer being able to tolerate their snooty attitudes, Peggy insulted Ally and the mothers as "a bunch of losers" and promptly stormed away, returning to hang out withWendyand other friends.

Soon, as the boys' practice session continued, Ally spoke with another mother about how SUVs were far superior to pickup trucks, reasoning that being in an SUV would increase her chances of surviving a crash. No longer being able to tolerate their snooty attitudes, Peggy insulted Ally and the mothers as "a bunch of losers" and promptly stormed away, returning to hang out withWendyand other friends.

characters.name: Alphonse Dauterive
characters.description: Alphonse DauteriveNameAlphonse DauteriveGenderMaleHairWhiteAgeUnknown(deceased)RelativesDauterive FamilyEsme Dauterive(wife)Gilbert Dauterive(son)Girac Dauterive(son; deceased)Rose(daughter-in-law)Rene Dauterive(son; deceased)Lily(daughter-in-law)Violetta(daughter; deceased)Emeril(son-in-law; deceased)Mr. Dauterive(brother; deceased)Mrs. Dauterive(sister-in-law; deceased)Bill Dauterive(nephew)Lenore Dauterive(ex-niece-in-law)Debut"A Beer Can Named Desire"(pictured)Voiced byN/AAlphonse Dauterivewas the husband ofEsme Dauterive; the father ofGilbert,Girac,Rene, andVioletta; the father-in-law toRose,Lily, andEmeril; brother toMr. Dauterive, brother-in-law toMrs. Dauterive, and the paternal uncle ofBill. He wears a three-piece suit consisting of a black jacket and trousers, a green waistcoat/vest, burgundy necktie, and a white shirt.

characters.name: Amanda
characters.description: AmandaNameAmandaGenderFemaleHairBrownJobStudent atTom Landry Middle SchoolDebut"Bobby Goes Nuts"Voiced byUnknownAmandais a student atTom Landry Middle Schooland one ofConnie's friends. She only appears once in the Season 6 episode "Bobby Goes Nuts". Amanda is a young Caucasian girl with a tall build, pale skin, long brown hair, and dental braces. She wears glasses, a yellow long-sleeved shirt, blue jeans, and violet sneakers. Amanda attended a sleepover party atConnie's house with several other girls. Unknown to her,Josephhad a crush on her. Inside Connie's house, however, she was quickly bummed out by the party due toKahnenforcing strict rules about what they could do and having to go to bed rather early. Sometime past 9:30 p.m., Amanda and the other girls engaged in a pillow fight until Kahn came in to stop it due to it being late at night, even confiscating their pillows as a result. Disappointed, the girls decided to go to sleep except for Connie, who urgedBobbyto come over and lighten up their sleepover since it was going terribly. When Bobby climbed into the window and accidentally ripped part of his shirt on a nail, Amanda and the girls were amused. Encouraged bySusan Clemmons, Bobby danced with his ripped shirt untilChane Wassanasongand his friends showed up. To her surprise, Amanda witnessed Chane punch Bobby in the stomach, but Kahn showed up and unintentionally broke up the fight, so Bobby and the boys had to leave.

characters.name: Amber
characters.description: AmberNameAmberGenderFemaleHairBrownJobStudent,Southern Methodist UniversityRelativesBobby Hill(hook-up)Debut"Return of the King"Voiced byGrey DeLisleAmberis a college student atSouthern Methodist UniversityinDallas, Texas, who hooks up withBobby Hill. She only appears in theSeason 14episode "Return of the King". She is voiced byGrey DeLisle. Amber is a young Caucasian woman with slender build, fair skin, and long brown hair. She wears a white short-sleeve V-neck midriff top, dark gray shorts with a red belt and white buckle, red and black ankle boots, and accessorizes with a black choker necklace, nose stud piercing, glasses, and purple lipstick. In "Return of the King", Amber patronsRobata Chanewith a group of friends and is impressed with Bobby's skill as a chef, however, is not impressed that he did not go to college. One of Amber's friends hears that the Alpha Alpha is having a party, where Bobby asks if they knowChane Wassanasong. Amber tells Bobby that everyone knows Chane, the fraternity's president. Another one of Amber's friends recommends going to a Beta Theta fraternity party, where Amber invites Bobby and tells him he is really cool.

characters.name: Amy
characters.description: For other characters named Amy, seeAmy (disambiguation).AmyNameAmyGenderFemaleHairBlond (Season 2)Brown (Season 3)AgeEarly 20sJobStudent atArlen Beauty AcademyDebut"Leanne's Saga"Voiced byN/AAmyis a student atArlen Beauty Academy. She is friends withSharona JohnsonandJill. In "Wings of the Dope", she provides hair treatment on a horse's tail with classmates Sharona,Kim, Jill,Lynne,Maria, andLuanne. Amy is a young Caucasian woman with a skinny build and pale skin. In Season 2, she has long blond hair, whereas in Season 3 she has long brown hair. She wears square glasses.

characters.name: Amy (Fun with Jane and Jane)
characters.description: For other characters named Amy, seeAmy (disambiguation).Amy (Fun with Jane and Jane)NameAmyGenderFemaleHairBlackJobCollege studentDebut"Fun with Jane and Jane"Voiced byPamela AdlonAmyis a college student who showed an interest in the Zeta Sigma Tau sorority. She only appears once in the Season 6 episode "Fun with Jane and Jane". Amy is a young Black woman with a skinny build and short black hair. She wears a necklace, a mustard-colored blouse, a cream-colored dress, and red heels. Upon learning of the Zeta Sigma Tau sorority on her college campus, Amy visited their building to learn about the organization and membership. As she listened toKathrynand two other women sing the sorority anthem,Luanne Plattershowed up and wanted to sing as well. Luanne sang "Lady Marmalade" briefly, confusing the women. Afterward, she and the other women were guided by Kathryn into the sorority's bedrooms. When Kathryn asked if they had any questions, Luanne asked if it was true that the organization was full of "sluts" because she could not join if it was true, making Amy and the others gasp. Luanne tried to mitigate the awkwardness by pledging "Zeta Sigma Tau", causing Amy to mutter in disbelief. She listened to Kathryn andElliebriefly discuss their college history and then heard Luanne try to fit in by claimingher motherwas once in a halfway house and the "big house", comparing them to a sorority house since she interpreted Kathryn's question literally. This caused the women to laugh at her, saddening Luanne.

characters.name: Amy (The Texas Panhandler)
characters.description: For other characters named Amy, seeAmy (disambiguation).AmyNameAmyGenderFemaleHairBlondJobStudent atTom Landry Middle SchoolDebut"The Texas Panhandler"Voiced byJillian BowenAmyis a popular student atTom Landry Middle School. She only appears in the Season 10 episode "The Texas Panhandler." Amy is a young Caucasian girl with blonde hair. She is seen wearing a purple shirt with a flower on the right strap, and blue denim jeans. She plans to host a party, making students throughout Tom Landry Middle School eager to be invited.BobbyandJosephwant to get invited, but their weird actions in an attempt to be invited get themselves snubbed. They wonder why they are not invited and why a new student is invited, and they see that the new kid wears cool ripped jeans that make him fashionable, making them want the jeans as well.

characters.name: Amy (The Texas Panhandler)

characters.name: Amy Edlin
characters.description: For other characters named Amy, seeAmy (disambiguation).Amy EdlinNameAmy EdlinGenderFemaleHairBrownAge42JobOstrich meat sampler atPink & WhiteDebut"I Remember Mono"Voiced byJennifer Jason LeighAmy Edlinis aPink & Whiteemployee who went toArlen High Schoolat the same time asHank Hill. She only appears once in the Season 2 episode "I Remember Mono". In 1973, Amy had gotten dumped by her boyfriend a few weeks before Valentine's Day. Feeling that she had something to prove, she spotted Hank, who happened to be walking in her direction. She pretended to have her bell-bottom jeans tangled in her bike's chain and pled for help. As Hank untangled her jeans, Amy claimed that her boyfriend had been killed in the Vietnam War in a ploy to win Hank's sympathy. After he managed to untangle her and helped her up, she managed to kiss him despite his resistance, after which he ran away from her. As a result of this kiss, Hank contracted mono and was bedridden for two weeks. Twenty years later, Hank's wifePeggyis asked to substitute for the recordkeeper atArlen High School. While there, her friendBeckytells Peggy that Hank had mono. Prior to this, Hank had told Peggy that he had sprained his back during those two weeks. Not being able to let it go, Peggy asks Becky if anyone else in Hank's senior class was sick during those two weeks, after which Becky tells her about Amy. Peggy tracks her down and goes to visit her at her current place of employment, Pink & White, where she works as an Ostrich Meat sampler. In the cold storage room, Amy tells Peggy what happened between her and Hank, after which she gives Peggy a coupon that will take 30 cents off her next Ostrich purchase. Peggy at first seems relaxed, only to punch a large hunk of meat hanging nearby. Amy backs off, thinking that Peggy will punch her next, only for Peggy to clean her hands on the butcher apron that Amy is wearing and leaves.

characters.name: Amy Pitman
characters.description: For other characters named Amy, seeAmy (disambiguation).Amy PitmanNameAmy PitmanGenderFemaleHairBlondeJobStik-TekemployeeRelativesBert Halverstrom(ex-husband)Unnamed childrenDebut"The Exterminator"Voiced byLisa KudrowAmy Pitmanis an employee atStik-Tek. She only appears once in the Season 5 episode "The Exterminator". Pitman was an adult Caucasian woman with a skinny build, pale skin, and long blond hair. She wears glasses and a red business dress with black lapels. Pitman was briefly married toBert Halverstromin the 1980s, and she has a few children.

characters.name: Andy Maynard
characters.description: Andy MaynardNameAndrew MaynardGenderMaleHairBlondAge12-13JobPlus-size children's clothing modelDebut"Husky Bobby"Voiced byPatricia ChildressAndrew "Andy" Maynardis a fat, bratty, plus-size model of children's clothing. He only appears in theSeason 2episode "Husky Bobby". Maynard is a male Caucasian child with a short stature, large build, pale skin, and blond hair. He wears a green button up tucked into long blue jeans with a black belt. He wears gray shoes with green shoelaces and a white and black navy officer or ship captain's hat. Maynard was shown to be very childish and full of himself for his age, pushing Bobby's giftbag off of the make-up counter. Maynard later told Bobby if he did not want to get into trouble, he would have to go get him a brownie. Before the show, Maynard began to cry when he realized that he would not be the star of the show.
